description:
A machine-learning engineer wants to feed a numerical optimisation solver that **only accepts
    a tab-separated file that contains feature columns but no header**.
    You already have a small raw CSV in your home directory:

        /home/user/raw_dataset.csv

    raw_dataset.csv contains four columns in the following order:

        id,feature1,feature2,label

    and exactly five data rows (header included).

    Your job is to prepare the material exactly as the solver expects and keep a tiny log
    for reproducibility.

    1.  Create a new directory called
            /home/user/optimizer_data
        (create intermediary parents only if they do not exist).

    2.  From /home/user/raw_dataset.csv extract **only** the two feature columns
        (feature1 and feature2, in that order),
        drop the header row, and save the result as a **tab-separated** file named
            /home/user/optimizer_data/solver_input.tsv

        Formatting rules the automated tests will verify:
            • No header line – the first line must be numeric data.  
            • Columns are separated by a single TAB (\t), not commas or spaces.  
            • Every line (including the last one) ends with a Unix newline (\n).  
            • Row order must match the original CSV.

    3.  Inside the same directory create a plain-text log file called
            /home/user/optimizer_data/generation.log
        with exactly **two** lines in this exact format (case-sensitive):

            Rows: <N>
            Columns: 2

        where <N> is the number of data rows written to solver_input.tsv
        (that is, the CSV row count minus the header).

    Summary of artefacts the grader will check:

        • /home/user/optimizer_data exists.  
        • /home/user/optimizer_data/solver_input.tsv exists and obeys the
          exact format described above.  
        • /home/user/optimizer_data/generation.log exists and contains *precisely*
          the two lines shown, with correct row count and a trailing newline
          after each line.

    A typical solution can be achieved with roughly three or four shell commands,
    but feel free to use as many as you like.  Display normal command output as you work.
truth:
<!--
        Files that already exist before the task starts
    -->
    <preExistingFile path="/home/user/raw_dataset.csv"><![CDATA[id,feature1,feature2,label
1,5.1,3.5,0
2,4.9,3.0,0
3,6.2,3.4,1
4,5.9,3.0,1
5,6.0,3.2,1
]]></preExistingFile>

    <!--
        Expected files created by the agent
    -->
    <expectedFile path="/home/user/optimizer_data/solver_input.tsv"><![CDATA[5.1\t3.5
4.9\t3.0
6.2\t3.4
5.9\t3.0
6.0\t3.2
]]></expectedFile>

    <expectedFile path="/home/user/optimizer_data/generation.log"><![CDATA[Rows: 5
Columns: 2
]]></expectedFile>
